reviews.review_description:
I LOVED this game at first. Told everyone I knew about it. Unlocked the first 10 spots, completed most of the challenges. But there are a few ridiculously hard challenges that will lock you out of the majority of the game. Namely the air time challenges at pipe, floaters at cloud break, and 30 second skeleton bay barrels. (How is that considered beginner tier??) I've put hundreds of tries into these challenges using every mechanic, setting tweak, board choice, you name it. 

The physics of the game need work. You slow down when trying to do an air up the face of a big wave. There are sections at skeleton bay that aren't makable if you want to stay covered, and every wave there sections. Carving is really janky, but I get that water physics of rail surfing are hard to simulate. 

I wouldn't recommend this game now until either the progression is fixed or the physics are improved. I would've loved to try the rest of the spots and unlock different boards, but as it is I'm perpetually stuck on "beginner" with no conceivable way to move forward. Lost count of how many 2.5 second airs I've gotten when I need 3, or a 28 second barrel when I need 30.

It gets two stars because the core game is good - getting shacked at big supertubes j-bay/puerto escondido/backdoor is awesome and doing 360 air revs at hossegor and trestles feels great. It just needs better gameplay progression and the physics tweaked a bit. 

TLDR: Overhaul the progression so players can experience the whole game, and improve the physics for big wave air time, carving, and sectioning barrels.

Love the concept and the look of the game but had too many glitches.  The surfboard was two feet off to my side a few times.  Needs more instructions. After the instructions they send you out to the ocean and immediately you need to get up and ride a wave, but there were no instructions on how to first start when a large wave is approaching you.  A few times I was able by accident to get up and ride for a few seconds, but most of the time the water just rushed over me and sent me back to the tower. I want to love this game, but the inability to play is holding me back.  I have well over 800 games so it's not that I am a newbie, but as someone who has never surfed in my life, the instructions need to be a little more complete.  Once up on the board, then what? Ride the wave until the wave disappears or you fall down?

developer_response.body:
We're sorry to hear you experienced some glitches with the surfboard placement and felt the initial instructions were incomplete. That's certainly not the experience we intend for our players, especially when a big set is approaching!

We hear you loud and clear regarding the need for better guidance, particularly for players new to surfing. We're actively working on expanding the tutorial to more clearly explain the basic controls for starting a ride, getting up on the board, and the objective once you're riding a wave (ride until the wave breaks or you fall).

Please also make sure you try the Missions which acts as an extension to the Tutorials.

Please also ensure “Endless Surfing” in Settings is turned off, which will take you back to the Tower where you can drop in on the wave by pointing rather than paddling.

We are constantly refining the game, and your detailed notes about the start-up sequence and objectives are incredibly helpful. We are aiming to create the most realistic surfing experience to VR, please note surfing is hard and takes time and practice. Might be worth watching a WSL competition to see how the Pros do it. Keep an eye out for an update soon! Cowabunga!

reviews.review_description:
Edit: I have found using “the thumbstick to steer” option to make the game far more accessible and fun.  Im progressing a bit faster and really loving the game! 

So much here to love but it is very very difficult making the game less fun and more aggravating The developers need to take seriously the option of unlocking all spots regardless of completing their near impossible quests or make missions a little less difficult. If you just want to have fun and enjoy surfing this is not the game at all! You will find yourself incredibly frustrated with what appear to be simple tasks. For example surf 500 meters or get barreled for 3meters with waves that aren’t long enough or waves that dont barrel. You must complete missions to unlock other surf spots. I would love to see the game opened up a bit with more “free surfing”

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the detailed feedback. We understand your frustration regarding the difficulty of the missions and the requirement to complete them to unlock new surf spots.

We designed the mission structure to provide a sense of progression and challenge, but we appreciate your point that it might be detracting from the overall enjoyment and "free surfing" experience.

Please note they are all possible after lots of practice.

We are actively reviewing player feedback and have already reduced the difficulty of the first 3 missions at a Surf Spot. Note you only need to complete the first 3 missions to proceed to the next spot.

Point noted re “free surfing” and we are looking at ways to bring that in sooner.

Appreciate the feedback @Volteric2026 hope you enjoy getting barreled at Skeleton Bay!

reviews.review_title: Great job on a difficult app.
reviews.review_description: I surfed for 30 years and this is excellent!  Turn off the padding if you have motion problems.   Surfing the longboard is also less disorienting and very realistic. Many thanks to the developers.

reviews.review_title: Please fix these issues to make the game
reviews.review_description:
I really enjoy this surfing game! The scenery is beautiful and the wave physics feel very realistic. Overall, it’s a great experience. However, there are a few issues that I hope the developers could improve to make the gameplay smoother:

1. Inconsistent detection for stationary rotations
The detection for 360° and 540° stationary rotations seems inconsistent. Sometimes I’m sure I completed a full rotation, but the system still doesn’t register it. This often causes players to get stuck on these missions. It would help if the detection were slightly more forgiving.

2. Jump / takeoff detection feels too strict
The timing window for jumps or takeoffs feels overly strict. A slightly more lenient detection would give players more room for error and make the gameplay feel smoother and less frustrating.

3. Mission icons need text descriptions
Currently, mission icons do not include any text explanations, which makes it difficult to understand the requirements. For example, the 360° rotation mission icon is not very intuitive. Adding short text descriptions would make missions much clearer.

4. The “10-second barrel” mission is extremely difficult
The mission requiring players to stay inside the barrel for 10 seconds feels almost impossible to complete. Adjusting the control difficulty or mission requirements would make this challenge more achievable for regular players.

Overall, this is a fantastic surfing game, and I really appreciate the work the developers have put into it. I hope these improvements can make the experience even better in future updates.

True Surf is an absolute pioneer in VR gaming and is incredibly underrated. It offers an immersive surfing experience that allows anyone to challenge the ocean from the comfort of their home—AC on, cold drink in hand, and completely dry! Like real surfing, the joy doesn't come from levels or achievements, but from the constant practice and the new creative sensations found in every wave. It’s addictive and never gets old.

However, while the concept is a 10/10, the controls are as challenging as the sport itself. I have a few technical questions and feedback:

Community & Tips: Is there an official Discord? I’m eager to learn how to better navigate the barrel. More tutorials or demonstrations would be amazing. Even with hints, successfully pulling off a move in VR provides a huge sense of accomplishment.

3rd Person Controls: It seems there are only steering options without explicit acceleration or deceleration. Is this intentional?

Physics Mechanics: Does crouching or standing tall affect the speed or balance in-game?

Height Bug: I noticed a discrepancy between "Standing" and "Sitting" modes. Standing mode makes the character feel unnaturally tall, which feels like a bug that needs calibration.

I'd love to see more interactive tutorials, especially for mission objectives. You guys are doing an amazing job—keep it up!

reviews.review_title: A whole lot to like. Impressed.
reviews.review_description:
I've been keeping an eye on the price and finally jumped in during the recent half price sale. 

One of my first questions was whether it supported third person playable view. Yes, a recent update has added this functionality although it seems somewhat experimental and hidden away in the options. This mode needs to be embraced, further developed and made more easily accessible. It basically creates the feel of a completely different game and will increase sales to those that may be put off by first person view and it's related motion sickness issues.

Third person view could really start to shine with some polish to the animations and controls (very limited control currently in this mode on True Surf). I really loved Kelly Slater's Pro Surfer on the original Xbox 20 years ago and feel the mechanics of the gameplay of that game would really hold up well today if they could be replicated in True Surf, it was just great fun. I suggest the developers get hold of a copy on PS2/Xbox or PC and really study why it worked so well. Being able to play a mix of Kelly Slater's Pro Surfer and True Surf third person VR view would be reaching Nirvana.

Regarding third person view animations, there's some oddities with the surfer's back foot often appearing to lift off the board and it looks like they are balancing on one leg. The board also sinks beneith the wave too much when low speed riding.

Water effects are beautiful and realistic. The skybox seems a little low resolution as do some of the logos on the boards when riding in first person mode.

I initially played in full motion first person view and yes you really need to have good VR legs to prevent you hurling from motion sickness. This mode is really only recommended for those who can ride VR Rollercoaster games. The addition of new comfort modes will hopefully go a long way to preventing motion sickness for those that cant handle the full motion mode.

Paddling speed is unrealistic, I feel like I have outboards for arms.

reviews.review_title: Real Life Surfer Who Loves This Game
reviews.review_description: I had to move away from the coast for work several years ago and can no longer easily go surfing. I don't enjoy traditional exercise. Surfing of course kept me healthy and active, but I never considered it to be exercise because it's simply so fun to me. I actually got into VR because I hoped it could fill the "fun and active activity" void surfing left. Gosh though, do I miss surfing. There's nothing quite like it. This app is about the closest you can get to it without getting wet! It's hard to describe, but it just feels right. I felt parts of my brain reactivating that had been dormant for years. What I think the game best captures is that feeling of "one more wave" that I suspect most surfers also feel.  One more run to try to get that thing you wanted to do right. I put the headset on expecting to ride one wave and see what the game was about for 5 minutes. 90 minutes later, it died on me while I was riding a wave. This is an easy buy if you're a surfer, especially a landlocked one!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for your positive feedback and support. We really appreciate it after years to get here, and to capture that “one more wave” is the highest compliment we could receive! A massive thank you from the entire True Axis team. So happy you can find your surfing flow and hope the northern hemisphere winter delivers at Pipe and Hossegor!

reviews.review_description: These a glitch in the game that says nambia is completed but it isn’t so now I’m stuck there and can’t progress in the mission it’s a cool game but the bugs are annoying
It was okay, it is very laggy and quiet hard and annoying to steer.

reviews.review_title: From a VR gamedev hobbyist former surfer
reviews.review_description:
I'm probably one of the few people on the plan
et who surfs and has (solo) developed a VR game (Rogue Stargun). I have to say this game nailed it. It's extremely difficult to program this type of game and get both the wave physics and surfing feel right. This game absolutely captures that feeling of gliding on water. I do not know how they pulled off what Meta could not. This is a true gem of a game

What can I say….I’ve found true love with True Surf VR!! Even after reading other reviews of how great it was, I still seriously thought this game would fall well below my expectations, as with most native vr games. I WAS WRONG!! True Surf is glorious, especially at the little things: the water physics (no small thing) when it reacts to stalling with your arm in the wave, pumping for speed, or turning your body by leading with your head into the wave.  It feels….real! Best Native Game in VR!!!  And it’s a workout as well! It’s only $19.99…get it now before they figure out what they’ve e got and raise the price!

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for the positive feedback and support. We really appreciate it and pumped you are loving the game!

reviews.review_title: Straight Port from mobile to VR
reviews.review_description: I've been wanting a surfing game in VR for years and this one delivers. If you've ever played the mobile game, this is a faithful Port to first person VR. Also like the mobile version it's a slow and rough start. Tutorial should probably include surfing lessons instead of just showing you how the controls and  work. The graphics are really nice and I can't wait to see all the different beaches.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the long time support and feedback. We have more tutorials top of the list. Hope you enjoy all the beaches they all offer something a bit different

reviews.review_description:
The new third person mode is excellent! This game is 10/10. 

Although now the hands are rotated...think yall need a quick fix for that

Original:

I got this game on release and hesitated to review it because it make me very dizzy and sick BUT the devs have fixed it by adding options! You can make the camera not 'roll' which will solve the problem for most people, and you can also make the camera static so you spin around in real life to match the board. These are fantastic additions but I would also love to sit on the couch and play it in a sort of table top mode - like the mobile version. A mixed reality table like Starwars Beyond Victory would be incredible, but honestly I'd be happy with just a third version view similar to watching the replays except i control the character. 

The feeling of being in an endless ocean and feeling the waves is incredible. Like even if you didn't want to surf the simulation of the waves is incredible... Heck I'd love a mode where I can just swim around in the ocean too! 

I'm really happy to see the developers are supporting this game and making improvements. Really my only criticism of it is casual players have to earn a good experience in this game by completing the challenges - you can't just jump in and experience massive waves - i see incredible videos of other players riding these massive barrels and the waves i get aren't nearly as impressive.... still they're larger than anything I've swum in in real life

Is there a discord? I saw someone made a subreddit for the game

I surf- and after about a good hour of being a KOOK- I just got the best barrel N MY LIFE at Skeleton Bay.  
  It’s proper PROPER legit.   I’m not kidding- this is the RADDEST VR thing EVER. 100/10.     Ohhh man I dig this so much- well done devs- u cats RULE

developer_response.body: That’s awesome to hear! We’re stoked you’re digging Surfa Rosa, stay tuned for the next update the graphics are going to get even gnarlier!

reviews.review_title: Amazing at first. Then becomes annoying.
reviews.review_description:
Idea is great and I have this on mobile…. 
I don’t think the developers actually played this in VR. There will be a point when you can’t complete the tasks. Hossegor is one example. Can’t catch a tube for 2 seconds that doesn’t exist. Caught tubes on other levels just fine. Easier to shred on iPhone. They need to change mission objectives as they become impossible later on. So much better as a game on iPhone and it’s free. This costs $20 and it’s
Frustrating as a game. 5 star reviews are from 5 minute quickies. Or people just enjoying the zen of riding out a long wave. 
 
Some suggestions for the VR version:

1. regular or goofy foot option when popping up.  The board always faces forward like your feet are together. 
2. Adjust mission difficulty some of the challenges translate better on touch screen vs VR. Or just open up all locations from the start. 
3. Needs a little more wider field of view. 
4. Board recommendations for current weather / wave conditions.would be nice. 
5. Waves feel small, allow for a crouch option as you’re too tall as default and have to make yourself a midget to catch tubes…. 
6. Needs a snap button. The touchscreen on mobile version works will for flicking snap backs, but the controllers feel Un-inspiring to use. I don’t get dizzy like some but it just feels off. A quick turn 180  snap button that quickly brings your view back the other way.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your detailed feedback; we genuinely appreciate it! It helps make the game better.

Game Difficulty: We've been developing and testing the game (and all the missions) for two years, and already players have successfully completed all missions. For example, achieving the 2-second barrel at Hossegor is very possible; it's a matter of finding the right wave.

Progression / Spot Unlocking: To unlock the next location, you only need to complete the first three missions at your current spot. The final two missions are deliberately more challenging, designed for players who have honed their skills and are looking for a greater test. We encourage you to focus on completing the first three missions to continue your journey. We aim to create an enjoyable progression system that guides players through the surfing experience rather than opening everything up from the start.

Controls and Mechanics: 
Stance (Regular/Goofy): You can pose how ever you like, just chose your pose, then hold the trigger then turn, then let go of the trigger change or you pose, then hold the trigger again.
Snaps/Laybacks: We have improved the collision detection with whitewater, making it very satisfying to smash the lip. We are actively investigating how to further evolve this to allow for moves like laybacks in the mobile game.

Technical Constraints and Future Improvements:
Field of View (FOV): We are currently limited to the underlying technology regarding the FOV.
Board Recommendations: This is a great point, and we will look into adding board recommendations based on conditions.
Big Waves: You'll experience massive barrels in game. Check out our socials to see what a pumping JBay barrel looks like.

reviews.review_description:
As a newbie surfer the one thing that irritates me is when beginner spots get crowded so that adds to the frustration when you are just about to launch and some guy is directly in front of you! This thankfully does away with that and once you get going it really does capture the sensation of surfing (I particularly love the foil board which gives you a sense of limitless possibilities as you pump to gain speed just like in real life)

I would give 5 stars only that the sim is missing a "free roam" option. At the moment when you fall or miss a wave you immediately get transported back to the overlook. I want to be able to stay out on the water, paddling out to the next wave and use my arms to gather momentum just like in real life. Keep the existing setup you have but expand it to include a casual free roam option where when you fall you can stay in the water and paddle to the next wave.

UPDATE: Updated to 5 stars after dev responded about endless surfing option!

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the detailed feedback! We're glad to hear that you're enjoying the core surfing experience, especially the foil board, and that the anti-crowding features are making a difference.

We understand your desire for a "free roam" option to paddle back out after a wipeout. Good news is if you go to Settings and turn on "Endless Surfing" you can paddle all day! Turn on Manual Standup and use both hands to push down on the surfboard to pop. At this stage duck diving is automatic, so you just paddle with pace towards the wave.

Happy surfing @evilStimpVR!

reviews.review_description:
Starting with a longboard is terribly boring. I think the mobile game also starts like this. Would be nice to start with a short board. No one wants a longboard. 
And it is the first game ever that has made me feel dizzy 😬 I don’t know why though. Never happened before.

developer_response.body:
Thanks so much for the feedback! We really appreciate you giving the game a try.

There was a concern that starting the game with a short board would be too overwhelming. Whether or not it was a good idea, so we decided to risk making things a boring with the longboard at the start while getting used to the controls, before unlocking other boards in the early missions. (True Surf on mobile does not start with a long board, but it is an early unlock)

We've added new settings which can be found in the tablet under Settings>Play>General>Surfing Rotation Mod which some people report as making people less dizzy. We will keep working on this for further updates, so let us know if it helps or not. In early testing, we got good feedback regarding motion sickness, but, now it seems some of our default camera movements really triggers some people.

reviews.review_description: As someone that doesnt get to surf as often as i would like this game is fantastic,the water effects are really realistic and i found the controls great,got surfing really quickly.there does need to be better tutorials for beginers,also would be great if the beach scenery was higher resolution.other than that im so glad this game has been made,thanks devs

developer_response.body: Thank you for your feedback! We appreciate you taking the time to share your experience, especially as an IRL surfer. We're stoked you liked the water. Have you tried stalling by touching the water? Point noted re Beginner tutorials, we will continue to iterate. Thanks for your support!

reviews.review_description:
This is darn close to riding waves in real life, the wave psysics are really good. The feeling of dropping into a steep wave is pretty realistic.
I feel like the shortboards have to much pivot. The Lightning gun behaves quite close to a normal surfboard. 

Don’t know how the experience is if you have never surfed, but if you surf this game is a must have✊

Hope the devs keep working on this title.

Potential improvements:
-Add rocks, shallow reef, riding close to shore, under piers, other surfers
-Grabs,laybacks, being able to stomp/kick the tail 
-Possibility to tweak board features, less pivot slower turning etc
-Fine tune controls to mimic real hand/arm movements 
-Real Surf technique training feature: Practice arm movements, head position

developer_response.body:
We appreciate your continued support and are pumped to hear you're enjoying the game!

We also value your feedback on the boards. Currently, we are developing experimental adjustments to the board physics. These changes include modifications to the center of mass, which will influence turning, and enhancing the surfboard's collision physics with the water.

We love the potential improvements you've outlined and will keep working on them.

reviews.review_title: Good game but mechanics could be better
reviews.review_description: I surf irl and this is a pretty good game with potential to get better however the air missions are a little ridiculously hard with the way the mechanics work and throwing your arms up to get air just doesn't work too well. In order to unlock other surf spots like pipe you need to beat the missions, which gets pretty frustrating. I don't know if the devs play tested it but it seems to me like they really haven't because some things are just ridiculously hard to do with the current mechanics. Maybe I'm missing something but right now it's a little difficult

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your feedback! We appreciate you taking the time to share your experience, especially as an IRL surfer. We're glad to hear you see potential in the game.

We understand your frustration with the air missions and the mechanics. Our aim is to replicate the challenge of performing airs in real life, and the 'throwing hands up' mechanic is designed to mimic a surfer crouching and hitting the lip to launch. Airs are not meant to be easy right away, which is why there is a learning curve built into the game.

Regarding spot unlocking, you actually only need to complete the first three missions at a location to unlock the next spot. The last two missions are intentionally more difficult, designed for when you've honed your skills and want to come back for a greater challenge. We encourage you to focus on completing the first three missions to move on.

We do playtest extensively, but we also constantly review player feedback like yours. We will take your comments on the difficulty and mechanics for the air missions into serious consideration for future updates to ensure the controls feel as intuitive as possible while maintaining a realistic challenge.

Thanks again for your support and patience!

reviews.review_description:
Update 7/1. Guys please give us custom analog (joystick) sensitivity control, like we have in the mobile game. The shortboards are too loose and hard to control and the best experience is with the single fin which is a touch too stiff. Either sensitivity control or a bigger board sitting in between the short board and the single fin. I’m on mission 3 teahupoo and only doing missions to see if I can unlock all the boards. Having a blast on the single fin in jbay and will be putting a video up. Side request: please give us a few different pre set conditions for the waves every spot. Bigger waves are more fun than smaller. Cheers

—-

Well I bought this game when I first got my  VR and had mixed feelings. Honestly I felt sick the first time I played it and nauseous but I kept trying because I’m a huge fan of the mobile version and a keen surfer.
I managed to figure out the sweet spot by only getting barrels and not turning much. Today I was being curious in the settings and found out there was a static camera option - game changer. This game really became fantastic and an amazing opportunity to surf and specially get barreled. I’m still struggling a bit with pumping down the line and I’m hoping that the right board will adjust that just like it did in the mobile game. On a side note: surprisingly this game has helped me with real surf barrel spotting just because of the experience of the VR. Amazing job from the developers THANK YOU.

developer_response.body:
We appreciate your support and feedback! We're excited to hear that the game is enhancing your real-world surfing and look forward to seeing your single-fin edit from J-Bay.

Regarding custom joystick sensitivity control—that's a great point, and it's on our development list.

We will also be incorporating motion sickness options into the initial setup experience for all new players.

Hope you are catching some of this big northern hemisphere winter swell!

reviews.review_description: I love the concept of this game, real world surf spots with both real world conditions and also doctored up big waves for extra fun, and all in VR! I just wish it was a little less grindy to unlock all the locations or at least had better tutorials. The way it is now it’s mostly just trial and error with no idea of what you’re doing wrong. Is there a Discord or some other social where players can learn from each other? Also I hate that metallic “crunching” sound you get when a wave hits and I have to set the audio to almost zero to avoid it. Still, it’s a fun game with a lot of depth (pun) and should only get better with time.

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for your support and feedback. We appreciate you taking the time to share your experience and we're stoked that you like the game. Point noted re tutorials and a hub where players can learn from each other, we are on it. Re the crunching sound we will look to include an option where you can turn it off. Thanks again for your support!

reviews.review_description: I overall like the game, but the hosseger break is horrible, if you are far out, muiltiple places crash down at the same time, so you can barely even surf, and if you go to far in, the barrel gets extremely small and then you are unable to barrel. Please fix this, i am on the last of the missions.

developer_response.body:
We appreciate your support and feedback, and we're sorry to hear you're not having fun at  Hossegor.

Hossegor offers punchy, quick barrels, which are quite different from the long, barreling waves you'll find at the next location, Skeleton Bay. To successfully make the barrels at Hossegor, remember to duck low and pump hard!

A good swell is hitting Hossegor this week. Have you tried using Live Conditions and boosting with the Perfect Storm feature?

Please note, you only need to complete the first three missions at Hossegor to progress in the game.

reviews.review_description:
Game is very aesthetically pleasing. The water looks amazing and so do the waves. Although it's plagued by its lack of realism. Paddling into a wave is a bit more nonsensicle than real life, and no real surfing moves translate to the in game movement. I tried many carves and cutbacks exactly as I would in the ocean and the board does not mimic the movement by any means.

However, I'm super excited for the day these issues are ironed out and we can more closely simulate ripping some waves in VR! Go team.

developer_response.body:
We appreciate your feedback and are excited that you like the water and waves! We are committed to continued iteration to simulate realistic surfing.

Currently, duck diving is automatic, but we have received player feedback requesting manual control over this action.

We are also experimenting with board physics adjustments, including modifications to the center of mass, which will impact how turns feel in the game.

reviews.review_description: I’ve always been a huge fan of true surf and grew up surfing in Hawaii my whole life so I was very excited for this game, graphics are good waves and environment look good but something about the movement and sensitive controls made me extremely motion sick and I’ve NEVER felt like that in any other vr game before and I actually had to play sitting down lol. Hopefully they’ll be able to smooth it out more or something but I’ll continue playing it maybe get my vr legs stronger to tolerate it longer.

developer_response.body:
We've added new settings which can be found in the tablet under Settings>Play>General>Surfing Rotation Mod which some people report as making the game enjoyable to play. We will keep working on this for further updates, so let us know if it helps or not.

Note that in our early testing we had reports from testers who normally got motion sick in VR that True Surf didn't trigger them, which made us maybe a bit too confident that we didn't need to add motion sickness options.

reviews.review_title: Get rid of 30 second barrel challenge
reviews.review_description: It is impossible to pass the 30 sec  skeleton Bay barrel challenge. The game is so fun, but that’s simple challenge is holding me back due to the wave literally closing out every section in front of you making you raise to the next section killing your barrel time

developer_response.body: We really appreciate your support and the time you took to share your experience and feedback. While the 30-second barrel challenge is difficult, it is definitely possible. Please note that to progress in the game, you only need to complete the first three missions at each surf spot.

reviews.review_description: I am still shocked how realistic the water graphics are!! Why isn't the endless surf mode is not the default mode?! This is change the all game!!! It make it extremely realistic!! You sould let people know this mode exist!!!! There are so many surfing spots!! And the missions are interesting!! This game is a master piece!! And more people must know about it!! But you have to let them know about the endless surfing mode beacuse it take this game to a even higher level!! Great job!!

developer_response.body: We're concerned endless mode is a bit too challenging as a way to start off, but it is a requirement for some of the later missions.

reviews.review_title: If they were all like Skeleton Bay.
reviews.review_description:
The gameplay and experience are amazing.  I grew up bodyboarding and surfing in San Diego and am now land locked in Illinois. This game helps ease that pain.

UPDATE! I thought this was going to be my forever surf game but the more I played the more I got annoyed and frustrated. 
I feel like I got my money's worth, but I finally hit a brick wall.

You see it started with losing progress on any mission you decide to take a break from or watch a replay. Some of these missions will take you hours. I must suck but I did rip through a lot of missions until Teahupoo...and YES it's the air missions. The randomness of wave types and the amount of them that will actually launch you vary so much it becomes a fishing game. The lack of explanation is real. I would love to see an example. I'm sure people can do them back-to-back but sadly I cannot.   I left the tutorial by accident and have no idea how to get back to see if the answer is there. 
They have you buy a mandatory board with most of your money at every beach but do not mark what board goes with what beach after that. 
The mission surf is better than the STORM less button which produces the same MIDS surf every time with no variation to speak of.  
After you pass all the missions on any beach it goes flat and is boring and ruins most spots. Live Surf is flat.
It's so sad we have no control over the surf we ride because it's the most beautiful surf I have ever seen in a game. 
This is the core of my frustration. 
That and you have to RESET your VIEW just about EVERY wave and it's killing me smalls. My meta button is wearing out. It should reset view on takeoff!
Skeleton Bay is amazing and the reason I return in short spurts. 
Every spot could be amazing. 
Just give us even the simple choices like Barton Lynch Pro surfing does for wave creation, easier and more realistic air mission goals, and fix the view centering thing please.
Then I would be back in my happy place surfing good waves.

developer_response.body:
We appreciate your thorough review.

First, regarding replays resetting your mission data: this is not intended. Please email support@truesurf.com with your device, operating system, and the specific mission where this is occurring.

We've noted your feedback on air missions. Airs are the most difficult maneuver in the game, and we are developing tutorials and looking into creating a community hub to help players master them.

The wave conditions are based on live data from Surfline. The "Perfect Storm" feature provides a temporary boost to these real-time conditions. With the Northern Hemisphere currently seeing large winter swells, we hope you get a chance to surf at Pipe!

reviews.review_description: Awesome, as a real life intermediate surfer !

This is truly everything a surfer needs.. snow outside, crappy weather? Grab this game and start it. Instantly enable paddle mode, please do it. And for the love of ... Make the adjustments in your settings, else you most definitely will get nausea, extreme level. But when it's fine tuned, this will be the best I have on my quest! Looking forward to Nazare, still completing some missions. I'm a real life surfer myself and really got to say this is so close to the real feeling!! There are however some improvements to be made, I'd love to have contact with the Dev's for these! Again +100 for the creators of the game, looking forward to more!

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for your support and feedback. We appreciate you taking the time to share your experience, especially as an IRL surfer. We're stoked you like the game. We would really appreciate your feedback as we iterate on the game, please send an email to support@truesurf.com

reviews.review_title: Please bring True Surf to PSVR2 in 2026!
reviews.review_description:
Landlocked intermediate surfer here...

This was my best spent €18 in 2025!
True Surf is my GOTY! Please bring it to PSVR2 in 2026! The PS5Pro will give it photorealistic water visuals!

The real life locations and real swell forecasts makes such a difference. This might be a game, but it is also a surf simulator. And it has alot of potential to evolve into perfection.

It is priceless to me to be able to experience the actual feeling of making turns on a surfboard on an ocean wave whenever I have 10 mins of spare time with my VR headset. The VR perspective while on the wave can not be accieved in 2D. VR and the turning with the body (arms swinging with hand controllers) makes all the difference.

Wishlist:
1. Bring True Surf to PSVR2! Pretty please!
2. Add surf spot: Nazaré, Portugal (maybe add big wave surfing sometime in the future too)
3. Please don't send us back to the tower instantly after a whipeout. Let us choose to go back to the tower by the press of a button, or stay in the whipeout if we want. Being in and under water is part of the surfing experience. Let us get hit by the wave lip or drop into the water... maybe fill the screen with a simple view of bubbly turbulent water for 1-2 seconds (to avoid having to simulate a true underwater view) before returning us to the surface. We can then pull in the board on the leash and paddle out of the impact zone and back to the lineup.
4. Visible body, not just the hands.
5. Oh, and please bring TS to PSVR2! :-)

developer_response.body: Maybe we could do a bit more work on the wipe outs, but, incase you haven't found it, there is an endless surfing mode hidden in the options, where don't automatically get sent back to the tower, and have to paddle to catch waves.

reviews.review_description:
This is my first review of a VR game. 
I usually don't leave reviews, especially not negative ones. But this time I can't help it. 
The game seems well-made, but perhaps for this very reason, when applied to a technology in which the direction of movement has a lateral component with respect to the direction of the eyes (like surfing), it has a serious problem: it causes severe motion sickness. And I'm deeply surprised because I've never suffered from it.
 I tried the game ten minutes before going to bed last night (the night before Easter), taking advantage of the discount period for the game I'd had in my library for a few months. 
Immediately afterward, I experienced nausea that lasted all night (disturbed sleep), and I still have it when I wake up. It may have been a coincidence, but I suggest anyone who wants to try it not to try it before going to bed, and if you absolutely must, not the day before when you have something important to do (but on the other hand, perhaps it's better for it to be a holiday rather than a work day). 
I can't even think of giving the game a second chance, the name alone makes me uneasy considering the nausea it's causing me.
I'm sorry, maybe I shouldn't have written a review so quickly, but that's what I felt like doing (I'm surprised but I feel really bad) and when I went online to ask for a refund I left a review.

The graphics are amazing and the wave is very detailed and you have real places and spots and wildlife it’s very fun and I didn’t get motion sickness at the start it’s hard but then you get it but one thing is that the mission a teahop’o is hard and I can’t get to pipeline which I really want

developer_response.body: Thanks for the feedback. Are you battling with the air missions at Teahupo'o?

reviews.review_description:
I love the true surf mobile game so I was so stoked on this. 
I am not sure if non surfers will love this but as a landlocked surfer this sure does scratch the itch. I still need to get a hang of the controls (they are a bit too sensitive for my liking but maybe I am emulating my turns too much) but the feeling of pumping down the line at a point as well as the scenery is spot on, so fun.
I surf Hossegor each year and it's the real deal. The physics just feel right

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for your support and feedback. It helps a lot! We are big fans of Hossegor too and love getting fast short punchy barrels! The controls do take time to get used to and hopefully we can bring the flow to some non surfers.

reviews.review_title: Good game but i have a problem
reviews.review_description: I cant stop randomly respawning and i cant play:(

Like most games, there's definitely a learning curve to this one, but once you start to grasp the controls the real fun begins.  Make sure to read the in-game help section that explains what to do to gain speed, turn, jump over waves, etc.  The devs recently added a few options to help those encountering motion sickness, which is a great addition and hopefully helps those that are having that issue.  It wasn't until a few hours in when I selected the endless mode, where instead of going back to the tower to select a spot to drop in after you've wiped out, you instead get to paddle out and decide which wave and what spot you want to try and ride on.  It's way more immersive and fun, and I've had some of my best rides doing it that way, not only in the barrels but also the distance I'm able to ride.  You can also press a button at any point to go back to the tower anyway, so you might as well use endless mode right from the start.  Anyway, I highly recommend this game for anyone looking for a fun, challenging, and truly addictive game.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for your detailed review and strong recommendation! We're thrilled to hear that the real fun began for you once you mastered the controls and that you appreciate the addition of our motion sickness options.
We value your feedback on the Endless Mode and are glad it has significantly enhanced your immersion and enjoyment. We will look to bring it in sooner.
Thanks again for your support and for highlighting the fun, challenging, and addictive nature of True Surf!

reviews.review_description: This is an excellent game!But their is one ask,this game can get quite boring playing by yourself so if this game had multiplayer I think it would be much more fun!!

developer_response.body: Thanks for your support and review! Multiplayer is on the roadmap! Dont forget you can surf live conditions. Europe has been pumping and so has Pipe!

reviews.review_description:
Is there a way to switch off the controller Buzz please? Control is intuitive enough once you have played a few minutes. Buzzing gets a bit much esp when one still gets a bit queezy from longer play. Like they buzz just by having hands in water and I always switch off haptic feedback on all games anyways. Just dont like it.

Big Ups on locking the horizon option...that made me able to actually play the game. I still feel bit queezy after a while but I think that is just the dynamics of VR as a whole. But it has made a huge difference that view is not locked to board direction anymore. Huge difference. Phew...

Btw. Uploading earlier I saw official WSL mobile game. Thank you since our friend YoYo Hopkins (as I'm sure you know) is officially in the 2026 WSL world tour circuit and you like other brands and companies make the circuit a possibility.

developer_response.body: Thanks glad to hear the horizon option helped and you're back out in the water! Hopefully we can get you to 5 stars.. Will add to the roadmap an option to turn off controller buzz. Yes True Surf has been the official mobile game of WSL since 2018 we are big fans of the tour and 2026 looks to be epic with the returning champs. Massive moment for Yolanda and Portugal becoming a rookie on the CT. Excited to watch her in action!

reviews.review_description: I've never seen such insane water effects on quest 3 real interesting game only problem i got being thrown back to the tower every time I wipe out is annoying as ive never surfed in my life I get wiped out constantly so I cant marvel at the water quality

developer_response.body:
Thanks for your review and support!
GO to Settings>General>Endless surfing and you don't need to head back to the tower!
You could also try taking out the foil!

reviews.review_description: It takes some getting used to (and I can’t recommend doing it sitting because you will probably get sick) but if you’re a surfer or wannabe surfer this is a must have

developer_response.body: We appreciate your feedback and support. We concur that the ideal approach involves maintaining a realistic surfing stance, utilizing the triggers and shoulder movements for turning. We aim to avoid joystick use to mitigate motion sickness.

reviews.review_description: Unfortunately, vr surfing is not going to be possible without getting violently nauseous, but it was such an amazing effort and try it could’ve been the best experience ever

developer_response.body: We've added new settings which can be found in the tablet under Settings>Play>General>Surfing Rotation Mod which some people report as making the game enjoyable to play. We will keep working on this for further updates, so let us know if it helps or not.

reviews.review_description:
The game overall is pretty sick, although the mission at teahupoo almost feels impossible to complete. I dont think anyone has unlocked pipe yet. Please fix this

Great game otherwise, i would hope to see more spots like snappers and margerate river

developer_response.body: Thanks for your support and feedback. Players have reached Pipe, however we will take another pass. Are you referring to the air missions?

reviews.review_title: Great if you want to feel sick
reviews.review_description:
No VR game should move your view without you moving your head in real life. Humans fix their view on a specific point even if our bodies are moving.

update after dev response: As an example, kayak vr had the exact same problem, but they added an optipn in the settings, accessibility menu, where you can turn off ''turn head in vr with the kayak'' and the game became perfect.

developer_response.body:
There is an update out with a couple settings added which can be found in the tablet under Settings>Play>General>Surfing Rotation Mod, to disable some of the rotation.

Some people found this makes things much better, let us know if that is helpful or not. We could look into adding an option to remove all rotation as well, or adding options to reduce FOV. In our early testing, we actually got a got a few reports of people telling us they often get sick in VR games, but found True Surf fine, which might have made us a bit over confident that these kinds of options where not needed.

reviews.review_description: Absolutely amazing, but should have multiplayer with public lobbies and private, body boarding/boogy boarding, proper falling, duck diving and snorkelling where you film your friends ￼

developer_response.body: Thanks for your feedback! We are currently focussed on any crashes and giving options to players with motion sickness, but will start adding items to the roadmap. Re Duck Diving - have you tried endless surfing mode?

reviews.review_description: I run the app and it says buy a board so I try but when I open the menu I can’t select anything and my triggers are working fine I also cannot travel around please help me

developer_response.body: Sorry to hear. Can you please send an email to support@truesurf.com with what Quest you have and operating system you are on. And what surf spot and mission you are on?

reviews.review_description: Super fun only a couple bugs. Barrel riding can be difficult some kind of pop up that tells you how deep or how close you are to hitting the lip or pulling out would be nice and not only for the barrel too.

developer_response.body: Thanks for your support and feedback. Great point we will look into a deep meter!!

reviews.review_description: Great tool for learning and practicing surfing.

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for the feedback and support. We really appreciate it.

reviews.review_description: At first it can be a little weird to get the hang of it but after is one of the coolest feelings I’ve ever felt in vr. There is also so much to do so you never get bored.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for your review and support! We really appreciate it.

reviews.review_title: brought a tear to my eyes
reviews.review_description: Barrels are almost as sick as the real  thing

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the support, review, feedback and tear! We truly appreciate it, it's been a long journey to get here for us!

reviews.review_description: Great concept, cool range of boards - really promising and hope it continues to develop. The actual controls could do with being made more realistic as opposed to holding a button down to initiate a turn. Main concern which I hope is an easy fix is that the view tracks with the turn rather than my actual head movement. It’s knocked me sick after about 12 waves.

developer_response.body:
We've added new settings which can be found in the tablet under Settings>Play>General>Surfing Rotation Mod which some people report as making the game enjoyable to play. We will keep working on this for further updates, so let us know if it helps or not.

Note that in our early testing we had reports from testers who normally got motion sick in VR that True Surf didn't trigger them, which made us maybe a bit too confident that we didn't need to add motion sickness options.

reviews.review_description: This game is the best and only surfing game on vr the graphics the physics everything is just perfect in this game trust me buy this game also first ever review

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the first review and for the positive feedback! Its been a couple of years in development and evolved from True Surf mobile in 2018.  We're stoked you think the graphics and physics are perfect!

reviews.review_description:
The game is very fun and enjoyable, but the stall mechanic is very frustrating and does not work half the time for challenges such as barrels, eh oh makes you happy out without being able to slow down and stay in the pocket. 

Also many experiences with glitches that just stop you from being able to catch waves completely and having to restart your game, losing any current mission progress.

A mechanic of savings mission progress would be so much more enjoyable and remove the frustration that glitches, headsets dying and other issues that are just completely infuriating when they lose you up 
to an hour of progress.

developer_response.body:
We appreciate you taking the time to provide feedback.

Regarding stalling, there are several options available, which you can find on the Tablet > Settings > Controls. Options include:

- Tilting both hands up.
- Lifting one hand while keeping the other low.
- Reaching both hands down toward the water.
- Pulling both hands behind your back.
- Our favorite: Touching the water with your hand.

We have noted your suggestion for a mission progress save mechanic and will be adding it to our roadmap.

Thanks again for your valuable input.

reviews.review_title: A RELAXING AND AUTHENTIC SURF EXPERIENCE
reviews.review_description: True Surf delivers a unique and refreshing VR experience focused on realism and immersion. Reading the waves, positioning yourself, and timing your moves feels rewarding and skill-based. The atmosphere is calm, immersive, and surprisingly meditative, especially in VR. It’s not an arcade game, but a sincere surf simulation that respects the sport and offers a great sense of progression. A fantastic experience for surf lovers and VR players looking for something different.

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for taking the time to write such a positive review. We truly appreciate it after the years of development to get here.

reviews.review_description: Go into settings there's a settings for motion sickness in general settings there's also endless waves so you won't go back to the tower the tower is easier to drop in you want to look at the top of the wave not down to the city alot of cool things in the settings area

developer_response.body: Thanks you for your review. Motion sickness and endless surfing are two of the most frequently asked questions.

reviews.review_title: It's a keeper, tips to reduce VR sicknes
reviews.review_description: Amazing port , great wave physics and graphics generally nice but as many have said it does make even a very hardened VR player sick. I played seated (as I'm in crutches) and found that you need to be rotating your head a lot to keep some kind of fixed reference, untill you do your turns of course then I whip my head round to the other side. It feels kind of like surfing bit that sidewards movement is the worst for VR sickness. Didn't see any comfort settings in my initial try either. A simulated helmet  or shades for some fixed ref point like in echo VR might work.

developer_response.body:
Thanks so much for your feedback! Main tip to avoid motion sickness is to focus on the wave. Interestingly, a few of our testers who normally experience motion sickness in real life had no issues in the game. Their advice is to focus on the wave, not the horizon. If you feel up to it, the bigger waves at Bells Beach might be a good spot to try this technique.

We've added new settings which can be found in the tablet under Settings>Play>General>Surfing Rotation Mod which some people report as making the game enjoyable to play. We will keep working on this for further updates, so let us know if it helps or not.

reviews.review_title: Never going in the ocean again
reviews.review_description:
Now I can just rot in my room all day and not 
Ever get I  the ocean. I just wrote a whole review and deleted it by accident so this will be shorter.i did get a bit nauseous playing the game and wanted to yak after 5 minutes. The graphics are super sick and it’s nice to surf Waikiki without 100 donkeys out. I wish I could log into my account with 218,000 clams and all the best boards after spending $16 on the game. 
If you live further then 10 minutes from where your surfing give up and play true surf vr. No one will miss you surfing real waves. This is better. it’s fun so I’d recommend especially if your a valley kook. Beat it kooks.
Also hopefully this helps my backside growing up regular in Santa Cruz

developer_response.body:
We're glad to hear you're enjoying the game and hope it helps your real-life surfing. We had better consider adding Rincon in a future update.

Re the Yak! A few of our testers who normally experience motion sickness in real life had no issues in the game. Their advice is to focus on the wave, not the horizon. The bigger waves at Bells Beach might be a good spot to try this technique. We are also going to include a static horizon option in the next update. 

The reason for the difference in 'clams' (currency) and separate accounts is due to the distinct progression systems. The VR version is a paid product and has 10 spots to unlock, while the free-to-play mobile version has more than double that number.

reviews.review_description: I like the game and all but oh my gosh i have never felt more horrible. I have never gotten so much motion sickness from a vr game, and i very rarely get motion sickness. So if your like sensitive to motion sickness DO NOT PLAY. But putting the motion sickness apart its actually a pretty cool game.

developer_response.body:
We've added new settings which can be found in the tablet under Settings>Play>General>Surfing Rotation Mod which some people report as making the game enjoyable to play. We will keep working on this for further updates, so let us know if it helps or not.

Note that in our early testing we had reports from testers who normally got motion sick in VR that True Surf didn't trigger them, which made us maybe a bit too confident that we didn't need to add motion sickness options.

reviews.review_description:
I bought game because very fin but pls add multi I have $3929 in my acc and I want to gift my friends game so pls add multi 
🐷🐷🐷🕊️🕊️🆗😭🆗✌️🆗✌️🫃🏿🥀🥀🫃🏿🇫🇮🆗🫨🤐🙄😪🙄😪😐👺🙀👺😹💩

developer_response.body: Thanks for your support and feedback! We have had a lot of requests for multiplayer, it's on the roadmap.

reviews.review_description: I hate the auto bail cause it keeps ruining my progress but it’s kinda fun

developer_response.body: We appreciate your feedback. Could you clarify if you are referring to the auto bail feature while the board is airborne? This physics-based trigger should only occur if the board reaches an unrealistically high elevation and should not activate otherwise. If you can provide a video of the issue, please send it to support@truesurf.com so we can look into it.

reviews.review_description: Absolutely great game I can’t wait for more updates, the barrels feel so good, the only thing is pumping and getting speed feels weird and you can’t play for to long before feeling like your gonna puke

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the fantastic feedback! We're thrilled to hear you're enjoying the game and that the barrels feel good.

We hear you regarding the pumping and speed mechanic feeling a bit off, and we are actively iterating on this to make it feel more natural.

Regarding the motion sickness (the 'puking' feeling), we understand this can be a challenge with VR. We've had a few testers who normally experience motion sickness in real life report that focusing on the wave face, rather than the distant horizon, helps immensely. The bigger waves at Bells Beach might be a good spot to try this technique. We also plan to include an optional static horizon feature in the next update.

reviews.review_description: Don't recommend. Underwhelming and annoying. Just abruptly transports you back to the tower after every wave.

developer_response.body: Sorry to hear you are not enjoying the surfing. We have had a lot of feedback about players wanting to stay out on the water, so are working on an option to enable that.   In the meantime, you can activate Endless Surfing on the tablet - Settings>General>Endless Surfing,  which will allow you to surf without returning to the tower. Or you can try the foil board!

reviews.review_description: Superb surf sim..better than expected .Full marks all the way. Nice 1!.. a wave sailing DLC would be very welcome :)

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the review. We really appreciate it. Will add wave sailing to the roadmap. IN the meantime have you trued the foil board?

reviews.review_description: This game is low-key sick but it would be better if it had multiplayer of some sorts

developer_response.body: Thanks for your support and feedback. We've had a lot of request for multiplayer, it's on the roadmap.
